Coding agents + sandbox forking

Prepare once. Fork the investigation.

State JSON Visual reference

Captured bug state

Coupon Bug Baseline

A coding agent has reproduced a real checkout failure and written the useful evidence to disk. From this prepared state, forked agents can test competing fixes without repeating setup.

No live run started.

Demo Step Checklist

Use this as the running checklist for the demo build and live run.

Forking Work

Loading prepared state.

Parent State Before Fork

Useful work is already on disk before a branch starts.

Multi-Level Fork Tree

The contract branch writes a candidate fix before it becomes the parent for refinements, validators, and the memory-state probe.

Lifecycle Steps

Stop, pause, fork, snapshot, and restore are kept distinct.

Why fork instead of clone?

Multi-level forks inherit intermediate work. Validators start from the actual candidate fix instead of rebuilding context from the original baseline.

Post-Fix Validator Fan-Out

A better fork point is one where the parent has already written useful disk state.

Candidate fix state contract-candidate-fix

    Memory-State Probe

    This probe attempts to fork after warming live process memory and records what the runner actually preserves.

    Fork Spawn Log

    Timestamped record of each parent, fork, sandbox ID, inherited state, action, and result.

    Evidence Panels

    Terminal-style output without splitting the story across several real terminals.

    Advanced Daytona Probe Log

    Output from the optional browser-started Daytona probe. API keys are not printed here.

    No live run started.
